prompt:
Implement flux issue #67: split the import flow so the Workouts empty-state CTA actually works with bare program files and old-app backups.

See https://github.com/gunk-dev/flux/issues/67 for the full diagnosis. Summary:

- Empty-state 'IMPORT PROGRAM FILE' button currently calls `applyImport()` which requires `{log, state, program?}` envelope â€” bare program JSON fails with 'Invalid backup: missing or malformed log'.
- Old-app backups have no `program` field (`exportData()` in pre-PR-65 `app.js` only saved `{log, state}`) â€” even a successful backup import leaves the empty state showing.
- No path to import just a program without touching log/state.

## Working instructions

- This is a multi-step refactor. Use the `[skip ci]` pattern on intermediate commits to avoid the pipeline auto-dispatching fix-agents. Final commit goes WITHOUT `[skip ci]` to trigger one clean CI run.
- Stay in your assigned worktree. Do NOT create additional worktrees.
- `git pull --ff-only origin main` first â€” flux PR #66 just merged so main has the new architecture (catalog, Workouts tab, etc.).
- Per flux CLAUDE.md: never reference Claude/AI in commits or PR title/body. Never merge.

## Changes required

### 1. Add a bare-program importer (`src/tabs/Workouts/logic/exportImport.ts`)

New exported function alongside the existing `applyImport`:

```typescript
export interface ProgramImportResult {
  exerciseMapping: { mapped: number; unmapped: number; total: number };
}

export async function applyProgramImport(raw: unknown): Promise<ProgramImportResult>;
```

Behavior:
- Accepts EITHER a bare program object (`{meta, phases}`) OR a backup envelope (`{log, state, program}`) â€” sniff by checking if `raw.program` exists.
- If envelope without a `program` field: throw a clear error 'No program in this file. To restore a backup with log + state, use the Import Backup button in the toolbar.'
- Reuses the existing `migrateProgram()` and `migrateWorkoutExercises()` logic for the name â†’ catalog id mapping.
- Saves the program via `saveProgram()` from state.ts.
- Returns mapping stats so the UI can show the one-time toast about catalog matches.

DO NOT read `raw.log` or `raw.state` in this code path â€” those are out of scope; this function is program-only.

### 2. Wire the empty-state button to the new importer

Find the empty-state component (likely in `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx` or a subcomponent). The 'IMPORT PROGRAM FILE' button currently triggers `applyImport()` â€” change it to:
- Show a file picker
- Read the file as JSON
- Call `applyProgramImport()`
- On success: refresh the program state (the empty-state will disappear once `loadProgram()` returns non-null) and show a toast with `exerciseMapping` info
- On error: show the error message inline (not just a console error)

### 3. Rename the toolbar button label

The bottom-toolbar IMPORT button is for full backups. Rename its visible label from 'IMPORT' to 'IMPORT BACKUP' (or similar). Keep its handler calling `applyImport()` unchanged.

### 4. Surface 'no program yet' guidance

After a successful `applyImport()` call where the resulting state has a log restored but `flux-db.program` is still empty: show a clear one-time toast or inline message. Something like:

'Backup restored: N entries imported. You still need to load a program â€” use "Import Program File" on the Workouts tab.'

The `ImportResult` already has `programApplied: boolean`. If `programApplied` is false AND the import succeeded with at least one log entry, surface this guidance.

### 5. Tests

Add to `tests/programSchema.spec.ts` (or a new `tests/import.spec.ts` â€” your call, whichever fits):

- **bareProgramImport**: load a fixture that is just `{meta, phases}` (use the existing valid program fixture or copy/adapt it). Call `applyProgramImport`. Assert program is in flux-db with expected phases.
- **envelopeWithProgram**: load a fixture that is `{log: {}, state: {...}, program: {...}}`. Call `applyProgramImport`. Assert only the program was applied (log unchanged).
- **envelopeWithoutProgram**: load a fixture that is `{log: {}, state: {...}}` (no program). Call `applyProgramImport`. Assert it throws with the helpful error message.
- **importBackupNoProgramShowsGuidance**: integration-style â€” use `applyImport()` with a no-program backup, assert the result has `programApplied: false` and `imported > 0`.

Add fixture files under `tests/fixtures/` as needed: `program-bare.json`, `backup-no-program.json`.

### 6. PR description

Title: 'workouts: split program import from backup import'
Body: reference issue #67. Brief on the three problems and the fix. Acceptance criteria from the issue, with checkboxes ticked. Don't reference Claude/AI.

## Acceptance criteria (verify before final commit)

- [ ] `applyProgramImport()` exported from `exportImport.ts`
- [ ] Empty-state CTA calls the new importer; bare-program JSON files load successfully
- [ ] Empty-state CTA also accepts backup envelopes (extracts the program field)
- [ ] Toolbar IMPORT button is renamed 'IMPORT BACKUP' (visible label)
- [ ] Backup-without-program imports surface clear guidance to load a program
- [ ] All new tests pass
- [ ] `nix develop --command npm run typecheck` clean
- [ ] `nix develop --command npm run build` clean
- [ ] `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test` clean
- [ ] All intermediate commits have `[skip ci]`; only the final commit triggers CI

## Out of scope

- Designing the LLM-generated default program flow (PR 5+ of umbrella plan)
- Shipping any 'default' or sample program in the repo
- Other UX changes to the Workouts tab beyond what's described above
